questions.subject: Economy
questions.prompt: In the context of the Modernization of Command Area Development and Water Management (M-CADWM), what is the primary purpose of 'Irrigation Management Transfer' (IMT)?

options.text: Transferring the ownership of canal networks from State Governments to the Central Government.
Shifting the management of irrigation assets to Water User Societies (WUS) to ensure sustainable farming.
Converting traditional flood irrigation systems into commercial drip irrigation projects managed by FPOs.
Outsourcing the maintenance of SCADA systems to private technology firms for a period of five years.

questions.explanation: Irrigation Management Transfer (IMT) refers to the transfer of management responsibilities for irrigation assets to Water User Societies (WUS). This is intended to promote sustainable farming and local participation. These societies also receive handholding support for five years to connect with economic entities like Farmer Producer Organizations (FPOs).
questions.takeaway: IMT is a decentralization strategy in irrigation management aimed at empowering local Water User Societies.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Sustainable farming by Irrigation Management Transfer (IMT) to Water User Society (WUS) for management of irrigation assets. WUS will receive handholding support for the next five years, helping them connect with economic entities like Farmer Producer Organizations (FPOs).
